title: Quiz: The Schrödinger Equation for Molecules
description: Test your understanding of the molecular Schrödinger equation and Hamiltonian

questions.question: Which term in the molecular Hamiltonian makes the Schrödinger equation impossible to solve exactly for molecules?
questions.explanation: The electron-electron repulsion V_ee = Σ 1/r_ij couples all electrons together, preventing separation of variables. This creates the 'many-body problem' that makes exact solutions impossible for more than one electron.

questions.question: How many electron-electron repulsion terms are in the Hamiltonian for a 10-electron system?
questions.explanation: The number of electron pairs is N(N-1)/2 = 10×9/2 = 45. Each pair contributes one 1/r_ij term to the Hamiltonian.

questions.question: In atomic units, what is the value of 1 Hartree in electron volts (eV)?
questions.explanation: 1 Hartree = 27.211 eV. This is twice the ionization energy of hydrogen (13.6 eV). The Hartree is the fundamental unit of energy in atomic units.

questions.question: According to the virial theorem at equilibrium, how are kinetic (T) and potential (V) energy related?
questions.explanation: The virial theorem states that 2⟨T⟩ = -⟨V⟩ at equilibrium. This means the total energy E = T + V = -T = V/2. The kinetic energy equals minus the total energy!

questions.question: The wavefunction for an N-electron molecule depends on 3N spatial coordinates (ignoring spin).
questions.explanation: True! Each electron has 3 spatial coordinates (x, y, z), so Ψ = Ψ(r₁, r₂, ..., rN) = Ψ(x₁, y₁, z₁, x₂, y₂, z₂, ...). This '3N-dimensional' nature causes the exponential scaling problem for classical computers.

questions.question: What does the V_ne term in the Hamiltonian represent?
questions.explanation: V_ne represents the Coulombic attraction between the positively charged nuclei and the negatively charged electrons. It has a negative sign in the Hamiltonian because it lowers the energy (stabilizing).

questions.question: Why is the hydrogen atom special in quantum chemistry?

options.text: It's the only atom with an exact analytical solution

questions.explanation: The hydrogen atom (and hydrogen-like ions) can be solved exactly because there's only one electron—no V_ee term. The solution involves separation of variables in spherical coordinates, giving the familiar s, p, d, f orbitals.

questions.question: Which of the following are true about the molecular Hamiltonian? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Its eigenvalues are the possible energies of the system
It can be separated into nuclear and electronic parts exactly
It contains both kinetic and potential energy terms

questions.explanation: a) True: The Hamiltonian is Hermitian, ensuring real energy eigenvalues. b) True: Ĥψ = Eψ gives energies as eigenvalues. c) False: Nuclear and electronic motion are coupled; separation requires the Born-Oppenheimer approximation. d) True: Ĥ = T + V.
